name: Byte Beat
explanation: A gritty 8-bit lo-fi drum machine in the E-mu Drumulator lineage: the crunchy, dusty early-80s sampling drum sound beloved of hip-hop. Six synthesised voices (kick, snare, hat, clap, cowbell, tom) run through a global BIT-CRUSH plus sample-rate reducer that gives the signature 8-bit grit. Like the original it is a preset PATTERN box: hold a note to start an internal 16-step boom-bap groove, release to stop and let the tails ring. Controls: Tune (drum pitch), Decay, Crunch (bit-depth and downsample grit), Hat (brightness), Accent and Level.
